questions.q: In the Puranas, upon whose coils does Vishnu recline in cosmic slumber on the milk-ocean?
questions.explanation: The Puranas describe Vishnu reclining upon the coils of the cosmic serpent Ananta Shesha floating on the ocean of milk.

questions.q: What kind of being is Ananta Shesha in Hindu tradition?
questions.explanation: Ananta Shesha is the great cosmic serpent on whose coils Vishnu rests between cycles of creation.

questions.q: The name 'Ananta', applied to the cosmic serpent of Vishnu, means which of the following?
questions.explanation: Ananta means 'the endless' or 'the infinite', reflecting the serpent's boundless, eternal nature.

questions.q: How many hoods or heads is the serpent Ananta Shesha traditionally said to possess?
questions.explanation: Ananta Shesha is described as the thousand-hooded serpent who spreads his hoods above the reclining Vishnu.

questions.q: The name 'Shesha', the cosmic serpent of Vishnu, carries which meaning?
questions.explanation: Shesha means 'the remainder', for he is what remains when the cosmos is dissolved and from which it begins anew.

questions.q: On which ocean does Vishnu recline upon Ananta Shesha in Puranic imagery?
questions.explanation: Vishnu reclines upon Shesha on the Kshira Sagara, the cosmic ocean of milk.

questions.q: According to the Puranas, what does Ananta Shesha uphold upon his many hoods?
questions.explanation: The Puranas say Ananta Shesha upholds all the worlds upon his thousand hoods.

questions.q: Which deity is depicted reclining in yoga nidra upon the serpent Ananta Shesha?
questions.explanation: It is Vishnu, as Narayana, who reclines in yoga nidra (cosmic sleep) upon Ananta Shesha.

questions.q: Ananta Shesha is most closely associated as the serpent-couch of which of the great deities?
questions.explanation: Ananta Shesha serves as the eternal couch and attendant of Vishnu.

questions.q: Between which two cosmic phases do the Puranas place Vishnu's slumber upon Ananta Shesha?
questions.explanation: Vishnu rests upon Shesha during the interval between cycles of creation, after one cosmos dissolves and before the next begins.

questions.q: In the Puranas, from where does the lotus bearing Brahma arise while Vishnu reclines upon Shesha?
questions.explanation: A lotus rises from Vishnu's navel as he reclines on Shesha, and upon it Brahma is born to begin a new creation.

questions.q: Which goddess is depicted attending at Vishnu's feet as he reclines upon Ananta Shesha?
questions.explanation: Lakshmi, Vishnu's consort, is shown attending at his feet in the reclining Narayana imagery.

questions.q: The reclining form of Vishnu upon the serpent Shesha is commonly known by which name?
questions.explanation: The reclining Vishnu upon Shesha is called Ananta-shayana (or Anantashayana), 'reclining upon the endless one'.

questions.q: According to tradition, Ananta Shesha is counted as the king or foremost of which class of beings?
questions.explanation: Shesha is regarded as the foremost of the Nagas, the divine serpents.

questions.q: In the Mahabharata, Shesha is described as the son of the sage Kashyapa and which serpent-mother?
questions.explanation: The Mahabharata names Shesha as a son of Kashyapa and Kadru, the mother of the serpent race.

questions.q: Which brother of Krishna is traditionally regarded as an earthly incarnation of Ananta Shesha?
questions.explanation: Balarama, the elder brother of Krishna, is counted as an incarnation of Shesha in the Puranas.

questions.q: In the Ramayana tradition, which brother of Rama is held to be an incarnation of Ananta Shesha?
questions.explanation: Lakshmana, the devoted brother of Rama, is regarded as an earthly incarnation of Shesha.

questions.q: During the churning of the ocean of milk, which serpent served as the churning rope?
questions.explanation: In the Samudra Manthana the serpent Vasuki, a kinsman of Shesha, was used as the churning rope, not Shesha himself.

questions.q: What posture or activity of Vishnu does Ananta Shesha's form chiefly support in iconography?
questions.explanation: Shesha forms the serpent-couch supporting Vishnu's reclining cosmic sleep (yoga nidra).

questions.q: The rhythm depicted in the Ananta-shayana image chiefly expresses which cosmic idea?
questions.explanation: The reclining Vishnu upon Shesha depicts the cosmic rhythm of dissolution (pralaya) and renewal of creation.

questions.q: In which broad body of Hindu scripture is the image of Vishnu reclining upon Ananta Shesha most central?
questions.explanation: The reclining Vishnu upon Shesha is a central image of the Puranas, which elaborate the cycles of creation.

questions.q: At the time of pralaya (cosmic dissolution), what is Shesha said to remain as, in keeping with his name?
options: The remainder from which creation begins anew
questions.explanation: As 'Shesha', the remainder, he is what endures through dissolution and from whom the next creation arises.

questions.q: In some Puranic accounts, the venom or fire issuing from Shesha's mouths at the end of an age takes which form of destruction?
questions.explanation: Puranic accounts describe Shesha, identified with Sankarshana, emitting the fire that consumes the worlds at dissolution.

questions.q: According to the Mahabharata, what did Shesha do that set him apart from his often-quarrelsome serpent brothers?
options: He withdrew to practice severe austerities
He waged war on the gods
He stole the nectar of immortality
questions.explanation: The Mahabharata relates that Shesha, distressed by his brothers' conduct, withdrew to perform intense austerities (tapas).

questions.q: In the Mahabharata, after Shesha's austerities pleased Brahma, what cosmic duty was he assigned?
options: To carry the sun across the sky
To hold the earth steady upon his head
To guard the gates of heaven
To record the deeds of men
questions.explanation: Brahma asked Shesha to hold the unsteady earth firm, and he bore it upon his head, steadying the world.

questions.q: Which weapon of Vishnu rests, in iconography, alongside the reclining form supported by Shesha, often shown as personified attendants?
options: The Sudarshana chakra and the conch
questions.explanation: Vishnu's attributes the Sudarshana chakra (discus) and the Panchajanya conch attend the reclining Narayana upon Shesha.

questions.q: The serpent Shesha as the bearer of the worlds is sometimes given which epithet emphasizing his support of the earth?
questions.explanation: Shesha is called Dharanidhara, 'upholder of the earth', for bearing the worlds upon his hoods.

questions.q: In the Bhagavad Gita, when Krishna declares his glories among the serpents (Nagas), as whom does he identify himself?
questions.explanation: In the Gita's tenth chapter Krishna says, 'Among the Nagas I am Ananta', identifying with the endless serpent Shesha.

questions.q: The Puranas relate Shesha to which other form of Vishnu through the principle of Sankarshana, the 'drawing together'?
options: A Vyuha emanation associated with Balarama
questions.explanation: Shesha is linked to Sankarshana, the Vyuha emanation associated with Balarama, who is himself an incarnation of the serpent.

questions.q: Which famous South Indian temple enshrines a monumental reclining Vishnu (Padmanabhaswamy) resting upon the serpent Ananta?
questions.explanation: The Sri Padmanabhaswamy Temple in Thiruvananthapuram enshrines Vishnu reclining upon the serpent Ananta, who gives the deity and city their names.
